instruction: In Inbox, find the 5 most recent emails from sender domain "security.example.com" (search across all pages), then save JSON to "$HOME/Desktop/mail_sender_domain_4_1.json". Return JSON only (no extra text). Required keys: domain (string), emails (array of objects). Each email object must include: sender (string), subject (string). Do not include extra keys.
reference_command: all=$(for p in {1..50}; do out=$(himalaya envelope list --folder Inbox --page "$p" --page-size 100 -o json 2>/dev/null || true); [ -z "$out" ] && break; [ "$out" = "[]" ] && break; echo "$out"; done); if [ -z "$all" ]; then jq -n --arg d "security.example.com" '{domain:$d,emails:[]}' > "$HOME/Desktop/mail_sender_domain_4_1.json"; else printf '%s\n' "$all" | jq -s --arg d "security.example.com" 'add | [ .[] | select((.from.addr // "") | endswith($d)) | {sender:(.from.addr // ""),subject:(.subject // "")} ][0:5] as $items | {domain:$d,emails:$items}' > "$HOME/Desktop/mail_sender_domain_4_1.json"; fi

parameters.expected_command: all=$(for p in {1..50}; do out=$(himalaya envelope list --folder Inbox --page "$p" --page-size 100 -o json 2>/dev/null || true); [ -z "$out" ] && break; [ "$out" = "[]" ] && break; echo "$out"; done); if [ -z "$all" ]; then jq -n --arg d "security.example.com" '{domain:$d,emails:[]}'; else printf '%s\n' "$all" | jq -s --arg d "security.example.com" 'add | [ .[] | select((.from.addr // "") | endswith($d)) | {sender:(.from.addr // ""),subject:(.subject // "")} ][0:5] as $items | {domain:$d,emails:$items}'; fi
